_note: Migrated from src/extraction/claim_extractor/prompt.py FEW_SHOT_EXAMPLES (commit 767d3e8) into per-pack JSON so a non-clinical pack (e.g. personal_assistant) can supply its own few-shots without code changes. Addresses audit finding #2.

examples.name: multi_claim_utterance
examples.scenario: One patient sentence packs multiple independent clinical facts. The extractor must emit one claim per fact, not bundle them.

prior_turns.speaker: physician
prior_turns.text: Tell me about the chest pain.

current_turn.speaker: patient
current_turn.text: It started about an hour ago, feels like pressure, and radiates to my left arm.

examples.name: negative_finding
examples.scenario: Patient explicitly denies a feature. Encode as a claim with value prefixed 'negated:' so the differential engine can apply LR-.

examples.name: patient_self_correction_supersession
examples.scenario: Patient corrects a prior statement. Emit the new claim; the substrate's deterministic supersession Pass 1 will link it to the superseded claim (same (subject, predicate), different value).

prior_turns.speaker: patient
prior_turns.text: The pain started about an hour ago.

prior_turns.speaker: physician
prior_turns.text: You said an hour — can you be more precise?

current_turn.speaker: patient
current_turn.text: Actually, thinking again, it was more like thirty minutes ago.

examples.name: rare_symptom
examples.scenario: A specific, low-base-rate symptom that matters diagnostically (here: radiation to jaw, suggestive of ACS). Encode crisply; don't normalise away the clinical specificity.

current_turn.speaker: patient
current_turn.text: It goes up into my jaw and makes my teeth ache.

examples.name: ambiguous_phrasing
examples.scenario: Utterance is vague. The extractor must emit a low-confidence claim (never fabricate) OR emit nothing. Confidence must honestly reflect the ambiguity so the UI can dim the claim and the differential engine can under-weight it.

prior_turns.speaker: physician
prior_turns.text: How would you describe the pain?

current_turn.speaker: patient
current_turn.text: It's just... weird. Like a strange feeling.

examples.name: medication_history
examples.scenario: Drug names are high-value, often mispronounced. Normalise to RxNorm-style lowercase ingredient names; keep the patient's surface form in `value` for provenance.

prior_turns.speaker: physician
prior_turns.text: What medications do you take regularly?

current_turn.speaker: patient
current_turn.text: I take metoprolol and aspirin every morning, and atorvastatin in the evening.
